options: The object marking is upside down
The downward slope of the bar marking should be towards the carriageway
The object marking should be black and white

options: When the speed of the motorcycle is above fifty kilometres per hour
If the road signs specifically allow and the speed difference does not exceed fifteen kilometres per hour
Whenever the motorcyclist wants to overtake

682.q: No driver shall pull, push or drive a vehicle against the direction of normal traffic… (i) Except when specifically directed to do so by a traffic sign erected by an appropriate authority (ii) Except when specifically directed to do so by a uniformed police officer on duty (iii) In any case

multi.q: Which of the following is correct when reversing a vehicle? (1) The driver must maintain a safe distance from the vehicle moving behind him (2) The driver of the other vehicle must not move towards him before the reversing vehicle completes its movement

Which of the following are the duties of a driver? (i) Every driver must always drive the vehicle with due care and caution (ii) While driving, the driver must ensure that he is physically and mentally fit to drive the vehicle (iii) The driver must not use any GPS device (iv) The driver should remain alert and focused on the road and traffic and avoid any activity that distracts him

options: The driver must ensure that no loud music is played in the vehicle
The driver must not watch digital motion pictures or videos while driving

The driver must not watch digital motion pictures or videos, including route navigation, unless the vehicle is stationary

multi.q: When entering or exiting a vehicle, the driver must ensure… (i) His own safety (ii) Safety of other employees (iii) Safety of passengers (iv) Safety of other road users

options: Stop the vehicle as soon as possible
Move the vehicle off the road

multi.q: What is prohibited for a motorcycle or three-wheeler driver or rider while riding on a road?
options: Riding a vehicle with a passenger

multi.q: What is required of a motorcycle rider and passenger regarding helmets?
options: Where required by law, they must wear protective headgear or other specified safety equipment
Experienced riders may decide whether to wear a helmet
Only the rider is required to wear a helmet; passengers are exempt
A helmet is optional as long as the motorcycle has a sidecar

multi.q: When is the rider allowed to take his feet off the pedals or footrests?
options: They can do so whenever they feel like it
Only when road conditions require or allow it safely
When driving in a designated parking area

options: If you are emotionally distracted, you cannot concentrate on driving
If your attention is not on the road, it will be too late to react and avoid accidents
If you drive emotionally, you may drive more dangerously

multi.q: Choose the wrong sentence:
options: Passengers are not allowed in the back seat of the vehicle
Passenger vehicles are allowed to carry passengers

Seat belts must be fastened securely to provide maximum protection
The area that cannot be seen when a vehicle is moving in front
The area that cannot be seen in the inside and outside rear-view mirrors

options: The blue truck is in the red car's blind spot
The white car is in the red car's blind spot

The red car is in the white car's blind spot

multi.q: When is this dangerous?
options: When the driver of the red car changes lanes to the left
When the truck driver changes lanes to the middle
When the driver of the red car changes lanes to the right
When the driver of the white car changes lanes to the middle
When the vehicle in front of you passes another vehicle, you start counting seconds and keep counting until you reach that vehicle
When the vehicle in front of you passes another vehicle, you start counting minutes and keep counting until you reach that vehicle

You start counting seconds as you pass a sign and keep counting until you can no longer see the sign in your mirror

On slippery roads, the vehicle is more likely to skid
On slippery roads, cornering is more dangerous
In slippery road conditions, the engine can overheat significantly
Assessing the condition of the road ahead

Get out of the car, look around, and drive quickly
Check your mirrors and watch for vehicles on the road before pulling away
No, because the car is in the right lane

Yes, because crossing this line is not allowed at this location

Object marking is for one side only
All of the above are correct

Vehicles Y and Z should let X go

Both vehicles have the right of way
Neither vehicle has the right of way
Both the auto rickshaw and the goods vehicle

options: Check your mirrors to assess the traffic around you
Signal your intention to change direction
Adjust the position of your vehicle
Accelerate to make a sharp turn

multi.q: What should you do after checking your mirrors and signalling before making an action?
options: Change the direction of the steering wheel without looking again
Assess the situation and make sure it is safe to complete the action
Increase speed to make the turn faster
Proceed without paying attention to pedestrians or cyclists in the area

Mirror, signal, position, speed, look, manoeuvre
Position, speed, look, mirror, signal, manoeuvre
Adjust your lane position as needed to make the turn
Increase your speed to indicate your willingness to change direction
Check your mirrors again to see oncoming vehicles
Indicate your intention to change direction

You use your left hand instead of your right hand
You can open the door more quickly
You can check your mirrors before opening the door

(i) After driving on the left and giving the necessary signal, turn carefully to the right when you reach the place where you need to turn (ii) After giving the necessary signal, move into the rightmost lane in the direction of travel and turn carefully to the right when you reach the place where you need to turn (iii) A vehicle moving in the right lane after giving the signal to turn right should not overtake another vehicle on the left side until it turns right

Increase your speed, increase your caution, be ready to brake with your foot on the brake pedal without pressing
Reduce your speed, increase your caution, be ready to brake with your foot over the brake pedal without pressing
